description: CLI interface for the AmazonAssociates affiliate program

auth.usage_instructions: Use this group to configure credentials, inspect auth state, run auth checks, and manage profiles. Start with `auth status` for a read-only check and `auth login` when credentials or sessions need to be created or refreshed.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Configure credentials or browser session state for a profile. Use `--profile`/`-p` to target a named profile, `--force`/`-F` to clear ephemeral auth state first, and `--credential-type`/`-c` to scope the login flow when a CLI supports more than one credential type.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored credentials and session state for the selected profile. Use `--profile`/`-p` when you need to log out a non-default profile without touching the default one.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Use these nested auth profile commands to manage named credential sets for different accounts or environments.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new named auth profile from the CLI `.env.example` template. Pass the new profile name as the required argument.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a named auth profile and its saved data. Pass the profile name as the required argument and use `--force`/`-F` to skip confirmation.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Show the stored metadata for one named auth profile. Pass the profile name as the required argument and add `--table`/`-t` for table output.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List every saved auth profile and show which one is the default. Use `--table`/`-t` for compact output, `--limit`/`-l` to cap results, `--filter`/`-f` for field filtering, and `--properties`/`-p` to restrict returned fields.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Show authentication state across profiles in JSON or table form. Use `--profile`/`-p` to inspect one profile and `--table`/`-t` for a compact summary.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run the CLI authentication checks without performing normal business actions. Use `--profile`/`-p` for one profile, `--verbose`/`-v` for detailed checks, and `--table`/`-t` for tabular output.

cache.usage_instructions: Use this group when you need to clear cached CLI responses and force the next read to use fresh data.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove all cached responses for this CLI so the next read uses fresh data. Use this after changing configuration or when cached output is stale.

program.usage_instructions: Use this group to inspect the verified metadata for the affiliate program or developer platform behind the CLI. `program info` is the canonical read-only summary command.

info.help: Show verified metadata for this CLI.
info.usage_instructions: Show the verified metadata for this CLI, including the official URL, Airtable record id, CLI type, and auth type. Add `--table`/`-t` for a key-value table instead of JSON.

usage_instructions: Use the `amazon-associates` CLI for CLI interface for the AmazonAssociates affiliate program. Commands follow the `amazon-associates <group> <action>` pattern; start with `program info` for the verified metadata summary and `auth status` for a read-only credential check.
